name: Josh Shapiro
bio: Incumbent Governor of Pennsylvania since 2023. Former Attorney General focused on budget deals, economic growth, and infrastructure investment.

name: Stacy Garrity
bio: Pennsylvania State Treasurer since 2021. Army Reserve colonel focused on fiscal accountability, reducing government spending, and economic competitiveness.

name: Brendan Boyle
bio: Incumbent U.S. Representative for PA-2 since 2015 and House Budget Committee Ranking Member. Champion of Social Security protection and transit funding.

name: Dr. Ala Stanford
bio: Pediatric surgeon and public health advocate. Founded the Black Doctors COVID Consortium during COVID-19 and served as Biden's HHS Regional Director for the Mid-Atlantic. Endorsed by Dwight Evans, Michael Nutter, Madeleine Dean, and 314 Action Fund. Running on healthcare equity, housing, and federal investment in Philadelphia communities.

q10.label: Support
q10.quote: I do support universal health care and Medicare for All. I practice it at 21st and Lehigh. If you have no insurance, we see you.

name: Sharif Street
bio: PA State Senator since 2017 and former chair of the Pennsylvania Democratic Party — first Black Muslim to hold that role. Son of former Mayor John Street. Endorsed by Mayor Cherelle Parker, Philadelphia building trades, and the city Democratic committee. Running on healthcare, housing, and progressive pragmatism.

q1.label: Support
q1.quote: I told them we could deliver Democratic votes for Sunday hunting if moms in our community didn't have to bury their children.

q10.label: Support
q10.quote: 500,000 people are getting health care through Pennie. When I go to Congress, I will work to restore ACA tax credits.

name: Mary Gay Scanlon
bio: Incumbent U.S. Representative for PA-5 since 2018. Former civil rights attorney on the House Judiciary Committee. Re-elected with 65% of the vote in 2024.

q11.label: Support
q11.sourceLabel: John Lewis Voting Rights Advancement Act

name: Christine Tartaglione
bio: Incumbent PA State Senator for District 2 since 1994. Labor rights champion and advocate for worker safety in North Philadelphia and Kensington.

name: Greg Bankos
bio: Republican candidate for PA State Senate District 2. Focused on public safety, fiscal responsibility, and small business support in North Philadelphia.

name: Anthony H. Williams
bio: PA State Senator for District 8 since 1999. Veteran legislator focused on education reform, housing, and economic development in West Philadelphia.

name: Nick Miller
bio: PA State Senator representing parts of West Philadelphia. Progressive advocate for housing rights, transit access, and wage equity.

name: Roni Green
bio: PA State Representative serving West Philadelphia. Healthcare advocate and community organizer focused on housing stability, transit access, and education equity.

name: Martina White
bio: PA State Representative for District 170 and House Republican Caucus Chair. Represents Far Northeast Philadelphia with focus on fiscal restraint and school choice.

name: Sean Dougherty
bio: PA State Representative for District 172. Elected 2024. Serves Northeast Philadelphia neighborhoods and has focused on transit funding and housing affordability.

name: Patrick Gallagher
bio: PA State Representative for District 173. Advocate for working families, public schools, and transit access in Northeast Philadelphia.

name: Ed Neilson
bio: PA State Representative for District 174 since 2015. IBEW Local 98 member and former Deputy Secretary of Labor. Champion for Northeast Philly workers.

name: Mary Isaacson
bio: PA State Representative for District 175 covering Old City, Northern Liberties, and Fishtown. Community organizer and former ward leader.

name: Joe Hohenstein
bio: PA State Representative for District 177 since 2019. Immigration attorney and former Temple Law professor. Advocate for disability rights, housing, and the environment.

name: Jason Dawkins
bio: PA State Representative for District 179 in Northeast Philadelphia. Focused on community safety, transit access, and neighborhood investment.

name: Jose Giral
bio: PA State Representative for District 180 serving Kensington and Hunting Park. Focused on economic opportunity, community safety, and housing stability.

name: Malcolm Kenyatta
bio: PA State Representative for District 181 since 2019. First openly LGBTQ+ candidate of color to win a PA legislative primary. Progressive champion for North Philly.

name: Benjamin Waxman
bio: PA State Representative for District 182 since 2022. Former senior advisor to DA Larry Krasner. Represents Center City, Society Hill, and parts of South Philly.

name: Elizabeth Fiedler
bio: PA State Representative for District 184 since 2019. Daughter of two public school teachers. Advocate for housing, safe schools, and solar energy access in South Philly.

name: Regina Young
bio: Incumbent PA State Representative for District 185 covering the Navy Yard and Southwest Philadelphia. Facing a 2026 primary challenge from Joe Sackor.

name: Jordan Harris
bio: PA State Representative for District 186 and House Democratic Whip. Serving South Philadelphia with focus on neighborhood investment, housing stability, and education.

name: Rick Krajewski
bio: PA State Representative for District 188. West Philadelphia progressive champion and co-founder of Reclaim Philadelphia. Focused on housing justice and economic equity. Facing a 2026 primary challenge.

name: Roni Green
bio: PA State Representative for District 190 in West Philadelphia. Healthcare advocate and community organizer focused on housing stability and transit access.

name: Joanna McClinton
bio: Speaker of the Pennsylvania House of Representatives. Represents Southwest Philadelphia. First woman and second Black person to serve as PA House Speaker.

name: Morgan Cephas
bio: PA State Representative for District 192 since 2017. West Philadelphia champion for healthcare, housing equity, and transit. Facing a 2026 primary challenge.

name: Tarik Khan
bio: PA State Representative for District 194 covering Manayunk, Roxborough, and East Falls. Nurse and first openly gay man elected to PA legislature from a suburban-style district.

name: Keith Harris
bio: Incumbent PA State Representative for District 195 covering Fairmount, Brewerytown, and Spring Garden. Facing a 2026 primary challenge from Kenneth Walker Jr. and Sierra McNeil.

name: Danilo Burgos
bio: PA State Representative for District 197 since 2018. First Dominican American in PA House. Chair of House Consumer Protection Committee. Entrepreneur and community leader in North Philadelphia.

name: Darisha Parker
bio: PA State Representative for District 201 serving Northwest Philadelphia including Germantown and Mt. Airy. Focused on neighborhood safety, housing, and school investment.

name: Jared Solomon
bio: PA State Representative for District 202 covering parts of Northeast Philadelphia. Attorney focused on consumer protection, education funding, and transit access.

name: Ben Sanchez
bio: PA State Representative for District 203 covering parts of North Philadelphia and Oak Lane. Community advocate focused on housing stability, education equity, and economic opportunity.

name: Art Haywood
bio: PA State Senator for District 4 since 2014. Covers Northwest Philadelphia neighborhoods including Mt. Airy, Chestnut Hill, and parts of Cheltenham. Consumer protection advocate and chair of Senate Democratic Policy Committee. Running unopposed in the Democratic primary.

name: Austin Davis
bio: Incumbent Lieutenant Governor since 2023. Former Pennsylvania State Representative from McKeesport. First Black Lieutenant Governor in Pennsylvania history. Chairs the Governor's Action Team on drug and alcohol programs.

name: John Ventre
bio: Republican candidate for Lieutenant Governor from Westmoreland County. Former law enforcement officer focused on public safety, reducing government spending, and restoring confidence in Pennsylvania elections.

name: Jason Richey
bio: Republican candidate for Lieutenant Governor from Allegheny County. Attorney and former Republican gubernatorial candidate. Focused on economic growth, school choice, and reducing state regulations on businesses.

name: Jessica Arriaga
bio: Republican candidate for U.S. House District 2. Northeast Philadelphia community advocate focused on public safety, fiscal accountability, and reducing federal government overreach.

name: Chris Rabb
bio: PA State Representative for Northwest Philadelphia since 2017. Democratic Socialist endorsed by AOC, Ilhan Omar, and the Philadelphia Inquirer. Running on Medicare for All, free transit, guaranteed housing, and a federal jobs guarantee. Gave up his House seat to focus solely on this race.

q11.label: Support
q11.sourceLabel: John Lewis Voting Rights Act Co-sponsor

name: Shaun Griffith
bio: Tax attorney and political newcomer from Roxborough. Worked in state government before opening his own firm. Running on Medicare for All, a $20/hour minimum wage, universal basic income, and community protections from data centers.

name: Larry Farnese
bio: PA State Senator for District 1 covering South Philadelphia. Attorney focused on neighborhood development, housing stability, and labor rights. Former ward leader with deep roots in the South Philly community.
